title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: contributors maintainers project, pledge respect people contribute reporting issues, posting feature requests, updating documentation, submitting pull requests patches, activities. committed making participation project harassment-free experience everyone, regardless level experience, gender, gender identity expression, sexual orientation, disability, personal appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, age, religion. Examples unacceptable behavior participants include use sexual language imagery, derogatory comments personal attacks, trolling, public private harassment, insults, unprofessional conduct. Project maintainers right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct. Project maintainers follow Code Conduct may removed project team. Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported opening issue contacting one project maintainers. Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant (http:contributor-covenant.org), version 1.0.0, available http://contributor-covenant.org/version/1/0/0/

title: Rmarkdown partial example
text: example, can make enlarge-able plots:

title: Rmarkdown partial example
code: library(rmdpartials) library(ggplot2) x <- rnorm(100) y <- x + rnorm(100) + 0.5 * x^2 curve <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x, y)) + geom_point() enlarge_plot(curve, large_plot = curve + theme_classic(base_size = 20), plot_name = "myplot")

title: Rmarkdown partial example
text: Values vs. fitted values.
code: reg <- lm(y ~ x) regression_diagnostics(reg)

what: Write a partial on the fly
title: Rmarkdown partial example
code: x <- 5 y <- 9 partial(text = "`r x` `r y`")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ruben Arslan. Author, maintainer. Gjalt-Jorn Peters. Author, contributor.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Arslan R, Peters G (2026). rmdpartials: Partial 'rmarkdown' Documents Prettify Reports. R package version 0.6.3, https://github.com/rubenarslan/rmdpartials.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rmdpartials: Partial 'rmarkdown' Documents to Prettify your Reports},   author = {Ruben Arslan and Gjalt-Jorn Peters},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.6.3},   url = {https://github.com/rubenarslan/rmdpartials}, }

what: Partial rmarkdown Documents to Prettify your Reports
title: Partial rmarkdown Documents to Prettify your Reports
text: Simplify using rmarkdown partials document objects R

title: Partial rmarkdown Documents to Prettify your Reports
text: Ever want make standardized way report summary something like regression, want limit one unformatted block text, one table, one figure? Rmarkdown partials allow define standardised knitr code chunks become part rmarkdown report. can turned HTML, PDFs, Word files. , example, create rmarkdown partial summarise regression models flexible way. can also define partials methods knit_print, rich rmarkdown partial made default.

title: Partial rmarkdown Documents to Prettify your Reports
text: Confer help : https://rubenarslan.github.io/rmdpartials/. See vignette quick example HTML document generated rmdpartials.

title: Partial rmarkdown Documents to Prettify your Reports
text: get latest development version:
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("rubenarslan/rmdpartials")

title: Partial rmarkdown Documents to Prettify your Reports
text: Define function like : create file called _my_summary.Rmd contents like :
code: my_summary <- function(object) {   rmdpartials::partial("_my_summary.Rmd") } ## My special summary  ```{r} summary(object) ```  ## My special plot ```{r} plot(object) ```

title: Partial rmarkdown Documents to Prettify your Reports
text: use partial rmarkdown report
code: ```{r} m1 <- lm(y ~ x, data = data) my_summary(m1) ```

title: Partial rmarkdown Documents to Prettify your Reports
text: make partial default printing option objects certain class echoed knitr document, give name knit_print method. Now, need let lm object echoed. can also preview result partial look like calling interactive session.
code: knit_print.lm <- function(object) {   rmdpartials::partial("_my_summary.Rmd") } ```{r} m1 <- lm(y ~ x, data = data) m1 ```

what: Convert text or file to a partial — as.partial
title: Convert text or file to a partial — as.partial
text: adds knit_asis class markdown chunk, can rendered viewer simply echoed knitr chunks. preserve figures unless path happens explicitly pass knit_meta argument.

title: Convert text or file to a partial — as.partial
code: as.partial(text = NULL, knit_meta = list())

title: Convert text or file to a partial — as.partial
text: text returned class "knit_asis" knit_meta can pass path figures resources

title: Convert text or file to a partial — as.partial
text: Returns input text class "knit_asis"

title: Convert text or file to a partial — as.partial
code: my_partial <- as.partial("## Headline Text")

what: Generate a small plot that will be enlarged in a modal when clicked — enlarge_plot

title: Generate a small plot that will be enlarged in a modal when clicked — enlarge_plot
text: plot plot large_plot larger version plot. defaults first plot left empty, works ggplot2 similar, base plots plot_name optional: specify meaningful plot name (needs unique document) width_small width small plot height_small height small plot width_large width large plot height_large height large plot ... passed partial()

title: Generate a small plot that will be enlarged in a modal when clicked — enlarge_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ if(!requireNamespace("pkgdown", quietly = TRUE) || !pkgdown::in_pkgdown()) { # will generate files in a temporary directory if (requireNamespace("ggplot2")) { dist <- ggplot2::ggplot(mtcars, ggplot2::aes(mpg, hp)) +          ggplot2::geom_point() enlarge_plot(dist, large_plot = dist + ggplot2::theme_classic(base_size = 18)) } else { graphics::hist(stats::rbeta(200, 3, 4)) dist <- grDevices::recordPlot() enlarge_plot(dist) } } } # }

what: Knit a child document and output as is (render markup) — partial
title: Knit a child document and output as is (render markup) — partial
text: modifies extends knitr::knit_child() function. Defaults change follows: environment defaults calling environment, passed, arguments passed via ... output receives class knit_asis, output rendered "" knitr calling inside chunk (need set results='asis' chunk option). defaults quiet = TRUE package additionally renders knit_asis objects viewer printed make previewing partials easier. achieved using rmarkdown::render() done temporary directory (used interactively/child mode). package takes care troubles behind scenes might find nest partials (trying resolve path ambiguities, using text instead files sources, functionality prevent iteratively overwriting generated figures files)

title: Knit a child document and output as is (render markup) — partial
code: partial(   input = NULL,   ...,   text = NULL,   output = NULL,   quiet = TRUE,   options = NULL,   envir = parent.frame(),   name = NULL,   cacheable = NA,   show_code = FALSE,   use_strings = TRUE,   render_preview = needs_preview(),   preview_output_format = NULL )

title: Knit a child document and output as is (render markup) — partial
text: input specify file path , read passed knitr (avoid working directory mess) ... ignored, can use clarify variables used rmd partial text passed knitr::knit_child() output specify file path , put file quiet passed knitr::knit_child() options defaults NULL. envir passed knitr::knit_child() name name use cacheing figure paths. Randomly generated left unspecified. cacheable whether results partial can cached knitr show_code whether print R code partial just results (sets chunk option echo = FALSE chunk rendered) use_strings whether read child file character string (solves working directory problems harder debug) render_preview true interactive mode auto-detected, false actually knitting partial child preview_output_format defaults rmarkdown::html_document() self_contained set true

title: Knit a child document and output as is (render markup) — partial
text: Returns rendered markdown class "knit_asis". used interactively, knit_meta attributes additionally contain path rendered preview temporary directory.

title: Knit a child document and output as is (render markup) — partial
text: default calling environment? Typically function defaults global environment. makes sense want use knit children context rest document. However, may also want use knit children respect conventional scoping rules inside functions e.g. summarise regression using set commands (e.g. plot diagnostic graphs summary regression nicely formatted). caveats: function return top-level. way cat() loops -condition without without setting results='asis'. can however concatenate objects paste.knit_asis() currently yet producing expected results RStudio notebooks interactive use

title: Knit a child document and output as is (render markup) — partial
code: # super simple partial example partial(text = "Test") #>  #> Test  # an example of a wrapper function that calls partial with an argument # ensures distinct paths for cache and figures, so that these calls can be looped in parallel regression_diagnostics <- function(regression, ...) {    partial(system.file("_regression_diagnostics.Rmd",            package = "rmdpartials", mustWork = TRUE),            regression = regression, ...) }
